neighborhoods.name: North Beach / Telegraph Hill
neighborhoods.notables: Italian restaurants, cafés, nightlife, Washington Square
neighborhoods.safety: Above-avg property crime (car break-ins); no open-air drug issue
neighborhoods.parks: Strong: Washington Sq, Coit Tower steps
neighborhoods.note: 2BR now pricier than your draft; great character + parks.

neighborhoods.name: Mission Bay
neighborhoods.vibe: Clean, new, convenient, very close to work
neighborhoods.notables: UCSF, Chase Center, waterfront, new apartments
neighborhoods.parks: Modern waterfront: Mission Creek, China Basin
neighborhoods.note: 2BR well over $5K. Modern but low character.

listings.address: Channel Mission Bay (Unit 438), San Francisco, CA 94158

neighborhoods.name: SoMa
neighborhoods.vibe: Best commute if office proximity matters more than neighborhood feel
neighborhoods.notables: Tech offices, warehouses, nightlife pockets, Folsom/Howard
neighborhoods.safety: Block-dependent; west/6th St serious drugs + theft
neighborhoods.note: East/Rincon Hill blocks far safer than west/6th St — vet the exact street.

neighborhoods.name: FiDi / Embarcadero
neighborhoods.vibe: Best possible FiDi commute with waterfront access
neighborhoods.safety: Embarcadero fine; FiDi core dead off-hours, car break-ins
neighborhoods.note: Empties out nights/weekends. Embarcadero edge livelier.

neighborhoods.name: Potrero Hill
neighborhoods.vibe: Sunny, residential, close to east-side offices, more space than core downtown
neighborhoods.notables: 18th St, views, hills, parks, quieter residential blocks
neighborhoods.safety: Generally safe; NW corner softer; some car break-ins
neighborhoods.parks: Strong & sunny: McKinley Sq, Potrero Rec, gardens
neighborhoods.note: Your read holds — nice lifestyle, 2BR over $5K. Hills + Caltrain access.

neighborhoods.name: South Beach / China Basin
neighborhoods.vibe: Very convenient if you want waterfront, Caltrain/Oracle Park access, and short office commute
neighborhoods.notables: Oracle Park, waterfront, condos, Caltrain, Embarcadero edge
neighborhoods.parks: Strong: new China Basin Park, Embarcadero
neighborhoods.note: Excellent commute; 2BR just above $5K at low end.

neighborhoods.name: Hayes Valley
neighborhoods.vibe: city energy, very walkable, gentrified, but could be close to less nice areas (Civic Center)
neighborhoods.safety: Core safe & lively; eastern edge near Civic Center/TL rough
neighborhoods.note: Your Civic Center caveat is correct. 2BR fits at lower end.

neighborhoods.name: Design District / Showplace Square
neighborhoods.vibe: Close to SoMa/Mission Bay, good for startup/office access, less purely residential
neighborhoods.notables: Showrooms, warehouses, CCA, AI/robotics offices, 16th/7th/Townsend area
neighborhoods.safety: Industrial/quiet; fewer eyes on street, some car/property theft
neighborhoods.note: Convenient niche lifestyle (One Henry Adams). Walk Score 91; sparse at night.

neighborhoods.name: Japantown / Fillmore edge
neighborhoods.vibe: Central, walkable, distinctive, and close to multiple strong neighborhoods
neighborhoods.notables: Japan Center, Fillmore, ramen, cafés, music history
neighborhoods.safety: Core safe; Fillmore/W. Addition edges need caution
neighborhoods.note: Good value + culture; block-by-block safety variance.

listings.address: The Fillmore Center, Unit M20306, 1475 Fillmore St, San Francisco, CA 94115

neighborhoods.name: NOPA
neighborhoods.vibe: Great neighborhood density with parks, restaurants, and central access
neighborhoods.parks: BEST: Panhandle + Golden Gate Park, Alamo Sq
neighborhoods.note: You undersold the parks — best green of the list. Bike commute.

neighborhoods.name: Noe Valley
neighborhoods.vibe: Sunny, polished, walkable, calmer than Mission/Castro
neighborhoods.notables: 24th St, cafés, families, sunny village feel
neighborhoods.safety: Safest-feeling; not near any rough area
neighborhoods.note: Don't expect <30m commute. Calm, low city-energy.

neighborhoods.name: Dogpatch
neighborhoods.vibe: newer housing, waterfront access, and east-side commute
neighborhoods.safety: Moderate; traffic > crime, some car break-ins
neighborhoods.note: Cool & walkable; 2BR low end only at $5K.

neighborhoods.name: Pacific Heights
neighborhoods.vibe: Beautiful, polished, very walkable, classic SF feel
neighborhoods.notables: Fillmore, Alta Plaza, mansions, polished classic SF
neighborhoods.note: Not walkable to office daily (hills). 2BR fits at lower end.

neighborhoods.name: Lower Nob Hill / Polk Gulch
neighborhoods.vibe: Dense, walkable, cheaper than Russian/Nob Hill proper, but a little more urban vibes
neighborhoods.safety: Borders Tenderloin; N of Geary fine, S gritty
neighborhoods.note: Best value + genuinely walkable. Which side of Geary matters a lot.

neighborhoods.name: Mission / Mission Dolores
neighborhoods.vibe: urban energy, food, nightlife, transit, and commute
neighborhoods.notables: Valencia St, Dolores Park, restaurants, bars, sunny weather
neighborhoods.safety: 16th St BART hotspot; Mission Dolores side quiet
neighborhoods.note: Best value + fastest commute via BART. Dolores side avoids the 16th St scene.
